meta.name: PT2 Domain Ontology
meta.description: Structured domain ontology for PyTorch 2 (torch.compile), mined from 6,167 GitHub issues (3,876 closed, 2,291 open), PyTorch source, and curated internal sources.

scopes.torch_compile: Core torch.compile stack (Dynamo, Inductor, holistic areas) — current focus (Phase 1)
scopes.shared: Cross-cutting entities used by all sub-ontologies

visibility.oss: Open source — derived from public PyTorch repo, GitHub issues, pytorch.org docs, public talks
visibility.internal: Meta-internal only — internal sources or attribution that should not leave Meta
visibility.confidential: Restricted — proprietary model names, perf data tied to internal workloads, business intel

lifecycle_field.description: Optional per-entity lifecycle metadata. Default status is 'current' if field absent.

schema.reason: Free text — why the lifecycle changed

provenance.type: pr | commit | rfc | release_notes | issue | docs

meta.changelog: v0.19.0: Component schema migration — merged deprecated_components.json INTO components.json under a `status` enum (active | paused | deprecated | removed). Added optional `replacements: [...]` field for non-active components. Added optional `loose_aliases: [...]` field to disambiguate phrase-aliases that need surrounding context (vs `aliases` which are safe for blind grep). Reconciled compiled_autograd duplicate (was in both active and deprecated files) — now single record with status=paused. prim_torch gets replacements=["decompositions"].; v0.18.0: Reproducibility framework lands (extractors/ + REPRODUCIBILITY.md) + testable_claims schemas declared for failure_mode and cause (closes Beaver's structural-validation gap from v0.1 audit). Splits Step-3 ask into type-level schema gaps (now defined) vs instance-level provenance gaps (filled by extractors); v0.17.6: Add MegaCache as optimization entity (compile-time caching mechanism in inductor that bundles FXGraphCache, AOTAutograd cache, and Triton cache); v0.17.5: Bulk visibility classification — tagged 243 previously-untagged entries across 11 entity files (backends, causes, components, deprecated_components, ecosystem, failure_modes, ops, optimizations, platforms, resolutions, user_journeys) as visibility: oss after content audit confirmed all entries derive from public PyTorch source, GitHub issues, public docs, or PTC presentations. Repo now ready for OSS push; v0.17.4: Residual internal-reference scrub — neutralized fbsource and fwdproxy mentions in METHODOLOGY.md, data/sources.md, validation/source_validator.py, skills/.../validate.py, skills/.../SKILL.md, skills/.../fetch.sh; Workplace group ID redacted. Functional code paths preserved for Meta devvm fallback. MegaCache references kept (legitimate ontology entity per Peng); v0.17.3: PII and internal-reference scrub — anonymized last unixname in experts.json, untracked 13 raw extraction dumps containing internal cross-refs (Workplace links, paste IDs, SEV refs, other engineers' unixnames embedded in path strings), replaced hardcoded /home/pengwu/ paths with $HOME / Path.home() / repo-relative paths in 6 scripts and SKILL.md; v0.17.2: Schema sync — declared expert and user_fix_shortcut entity types, added replaced_by relationship type, added lifecycle field convention to support doc-audit deprecation discovery; v0.17.1: Due-diligence scrubbing — strip unixnames, tag experts as internal; v0.17.0: Visibility classification layer added; v0.16.0: Enriched relationship layer (294 evidence edges, 62 triage paths, 12 entry points); v0.15.0: Relationship mapping (evidence edges, triage tree, component playbooks); v0.14.0: Phase 2 extraction (324 issues, 62 symptoms, 33 workarounds, 40 configs); v0.9.0: Full dataset download, Phase 1 diagnostic extraction; v0.8.0: Label mappings, expert mapping, visibility field; v0.7.0: STO owners and GitHub label mappings; v0.6.x: Beaver cause tree merge, Platform entity type; v0.5.x: User journeys, is_subcause_of tree; v0.4.0: Beaver entity extraction

entity_types.component: A software component or subsystem in the PT2 stack. Carries a `status` field (active | paused | deprecated | removed; default active), an optional `replacements: [...]` field (entity ids of suggested successors for non-active entries), and an optional `loose_aliases: [...]` field (phrases that match the concept but need context disambiguation, vs `aliases` which are safe for blind string match).
entity_types.failure_mode: A category of things that can go wrong
entity_types.symptom: An observable signal that something is wrong
entity_types.cause: A root cause that triggers a failure mode
entity_types.resolution: A category of fix or workaround
entity_types.user_fix_shortcut: A specific, named user-facing workaround — concrete advice users can apply (file: user_fix_shortcuts.json; also referred to as 'workaround')
entity_types.model: A user model or model family affected by issues
entity_types.ecosystem: An ecosystem project, benchmark, or framework that interacts with PT2
entity_types.optimization: A specific optimization technique or pass
entity_types.user_journey: A user-facing problem scenario that defines a diagnostic and resolution workflow
entity_types.platform: A hardware accelerator or operating system that affects issue diagnosis and support level
entity_types.expert: An engineer with deep knowledge of a specific component or area (mapped to anonymized engineer_XX IDs; always visibility: internal)

relationship_types.is_component_of: X is a sub-component of Y
relationship_types.depends_on: X depends on Y at runtime or compile time
relationship_types.fixed_by: X (failure_mode) can be resolved by Y (resolution)
relationship_types.diagnosed_by: X (failure_mode/journey) is diagnosed using Y (diagnostic tool/config)
relationship_types.resolved_by: X (failure_mode/journey) is resolved by Y (resolution), with fix_type property
relationship_types.routes_to: X (journey) routes to Y (journey) when diagnostic reveals specific root cause
relationship_types.enters_via: X (journey) is entered when user observes Y (symptom/failure_mode)
relationship_types.involves: X (journey) involves Y (component/config/op) as relevant context
relationship_types.is_subcause_of: X (specific cause) is a sub-cause of Y (cause category) — supports funnel/tree structure
relationship_types.platform_specific: X (cause/failure_mode) is specific to or primarily affects Y (platform)
relationship_types.replaced_by: X (deprecated/removed entity) is replaced by Y (current entity). Edges carry since_version and provenance. Optional — some deprecations have no successor.

testable_claims_schemas._description: Per-entity-type schemas for the testable_claims block. A testable_claim is a fact about an entity that can be verified against a source-of-truth (PyTorch source, official docs, error message templates, etc.). Beaver's audit pipeline (Step 3) cross-checks these claims against page content. Empty values are permitted — they signal 'not yet mined'. The instance-level provenance gap is filled by the extractors under extractors/; this section closes the type-level gap (no schema declared) by giving each entity type a defined shape.

applies_when.description: Conditions under which this failure mode is triggered. Plain prose, sourceable to a doc or source comment.

surface_signals.description: Observable signals that indicate this failure mode. Examples: 'log line contains "graph break"', 'torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported raised'. Each signal should be matchable against logs/error output.

affects_compilation_phase.description: Which compilation phase the failure mode surfaces in. Helps route diagnostics.

verification_source.description: Stable source ref where applies_when / surface_signals can be verified. Format: 'pytorch@SHA:path:line', 'docs:page#anchor', 'graph_break_site:GB####', etc.

triggering_pattern.description: The code pattern, condition, or upstream event that triggers this cause. Plain prose.

code_signal.description: Optional regex or AST pattern for detecting this cause in user code. Used by automated diagnosers.

surface_message.description: Error message template Dynamo/Inductor emits when this cause fires (if any). Mineable from raise Unsupported(...) call sites.

verification_source.description: Stable source ref where the cause's triggering condition can be verified.
